items.answer: *ma* (we two, excluding you) refers to "I" alone. The speaker went to the market with the other person, so Tongan uses the dual to include both participants.
*mo* (you two) refers to "you" alone. The listener will study with the other person, so Tongan includes both in the pronoun.
*nau* (they, three or more) may refer to just two people. Because the third person (the one after *mo ia*) is also involved, Tongan counts everyone in the situation.
